content.problemStatement:
A good number is a positive integer which is not divisible by the cube (third power) of any prime. For example, 222, 333, 25=5⋅525 = 5 \cdot 525=5⋅5, 63=7⋅963 = 7 \cdot 963=7⋅9 and 2057=11⋅11⋅172057 = 11 \cdot 11 \cdot 172057=11⋅11⋅17 are good numbers, but 8=2⋅2⋅28 = 2 \cdot 2 \cdot 28=2⋅2⋅2, 9317=7⋅11⋅11⋅119317 = 7 \cdot 11 \cdot 11 \cdot 119317=7⋅11⋅11⋅11 and 2401=7⋅7⋅7⋅72401 = 7 \cdot 7 \cdot 7 \cdot 72401=7⋅7⋅7⋅7 are not good numbers.

A pair of positive integers (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) is special if x⋅yx \cdot yx⋅y is a good number. A set of positive integers is nice if each pair of different integers from this set is special. In particular, the empty set is nice.

You are given a set of integers {A1,A2,…,AN}\{A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N\}{A1​,A2​,…,AN​}. Find the number of nice subsets of this set.
constraints: A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of nice subsets.
